title: Smoky Zanzibari Beef Stir-fry
description: A hearty and flavorful low-carb-friendly meal perfect for a sick-day comfort food dish featuring slow-cooked beef with smokiness from pickle juice.
instructions: Trim the sirloin of excess fat. Slice it into thin strips.
Finely chop the yellow onions, bell peppers, and garlic cloves.
Mix olive oil with lime juice in a bowl for marinade. Add sliced onions, bell peppers, and garlic to marinate overnight or for at least 30 minutes.
Heat some olive oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Sear the beef strips until golden brown on all sides.
Add smoked paprika and salt/pepper to taste into the wok. Stir-fry for a minute, allowing the spices to meld with the meat.
Once the beef is well-browned, add the marinated vegetables from the bowl. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally until the vegetables are tender but still have some crunch.

Finish cooking by adding a splash of water or chicken broth if needed to cook out any excess juice, and reduce heat to low. Cover the wok with a lid for 2-3 minutes to ensure everything is well-cooked through.
